# case159_constexpr_function_body_changed — `constexpr` function body changed

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Verdict** | 🟡 **COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK** |
| **Category** | Risk |
| **Platforms** | Linux |
| **Flags** | — |
| **Detected `ChangeKind`s** | `constexpr_function_body_changed` |
| **Source files** | `examples/case159_constexpr_function_body_changed/` |

**Verdict:** 🟡 COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK · **Finding:** `constexpr_function_body_changed` · **Evidence tier:** L4

> These cases ship a hand-built pair of evidence-model fixtures (`old.json` +
> `new.json`) instead of compiled `v1`/`v2` binaries, so the corpus is validated
> compiler-free by `tests/test_l3l4l5_examples.py`. See
> `scripts/gen_l3l4l5_examples.py`.

## What it demonstrates

The public `constexpr` function `demo::area` keeps its signature and (absent) exported symbol, but its body fingerprint changes between v1 and v2. Consumers that evaluated it at compile time (array bounds, template arguments, `static_assert`) bake in the old result until recompiled, so a mixed build can compute two different compile-time values from the same call.

## Why no single artifact layer sees it

| Source | What it sees alone |
|--------|--------------------|
| Binary (L0/L1) | nothing — the construct leaves no artifact footprint |
| Header AST (L2) | partial — but source replay is what records this surface authoritatively |
| **Source replay (L4)** | the per-TU source surface → the finding |

A `constexpr` function's result is computed in the *consumer* at compile time; there is no exported symbol and no runtime artifact to diff. Only the L4 source-replay body fingerprint sees the change. (Distinct from `constexpr_value_changed`, which covers a `constexpr` *constant*'s value.)

Per ADR-028 D3 a build/source-evidence finding never decides a shipped-ABI break
on its own — the artifact diff proves any concrete break; this finding flags the
elevated risk (or source/API break) and localizes the cause for review.

## How to fix

Treat a public `constexpr` body as part of the API contract: version it deliberately, and recompile consumers when its compile-time result changes.

---

- `new.json`
